item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This SCP makes me singlehandedly interested in Wondertainment as a GOI.
+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What about it?

  • It's a weapon that fires a projectile by using the gas pressure from the explosive combustion of cordite in an enclosed chamber, commonly called a firearm. Unlike Mr. Headless, you probably already knew that, though.
  • One of the people who discovered Mr. Headless had one. They fired it and shot one of the other people that had come inside with them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Someone explain to me the gun?

item.title: And... +100!
item.content:encoded:
Wanted to refrain from upvoting this myself straightaway, with it being a rewrite of my own draft. Figured I'd wait for a special time to do it.

Once again Voct, you did a damm great job rewriting my original draft.

EDIT: I'm so proud of my headless stepchild

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gadzooks, I really love the Little Misters. I think they might be my favorite "group" of SCPs. Some of the first I've read. I was terribly excited when I saw that more was added since I last visited their pages some years ago, and this does not disappoint! I do love his characterization and I find him rather charming and cute. To be honest, I was expecting Mister Headless to be some scary dude who stole people's heads to wear them himself. This is far better and original! He seems like such a unique fellow and he stands out well. You have an upvote from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The interview really makes this character. It makes me feel sympathetic for him and hope he's treated well at the Foundation. Of course, SCPs aren't needlessly mistreated without reason but he seems like he just wants to have a purpose and make some friends, or at least know what exactly he's all about.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thanks mate. it was the only picture of a headless man that wasn't either A) A halloween costume or B) watermarked

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'll think about it. Good work in finding it, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Image will be added upon approval from author(of headless) and/or admins(of this site) I highly recommend reading the story I found the picture on Friday Phrases. Great people.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Working on it, but from what I understand, Friday Phrases also licenses stuff under CC.

Edit: If I link to the original story that contain the picture and give proper credit, it will be fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Do you have the licensing information for that image?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Permission to add image

And will give proper credit.
image here

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Honestly one of my favorite Misters, not my most favorite but it's up there. I love backstory heavy SCPs with long interviews, and the backstory to this guy really tickles my fancy, a living halloween decoration? What a sad life to live.

1+

Also I should thank Tats Top Ten for making a Misters list so I can return back to these things, they give Wondertainment a lot more depth then just a "dude what make cool children toys"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great! Now all that's left is Ms. Sweetie!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Interesting.

The effect itself is a little lukewarm for me, but the interview is fantastic and the character you created for Mister Headless is so endearing and rather tragic, as is the case with many of the Misters. I also like the idea of this being a holiday themed mister.

+1. Nice addition to the list.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Effortlessly weird and oddly poignant. I really dig the Little Misters and this is my favorite thus far.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: At first I wasn't very impressed, but then I figured 2287 was headless in less conventional ways than the physical one - in fact, he's not quite real himself. He's got a physical form and he speaks, but the fact that people see him as headless versions of themselves, and the fact that he is able to wear headpieces despite the lack of presence of a head, and the fact that he does not experience a sense of self like normal people - primes me to suggest that there's not much to him left besides perhaps a bundle of taped responses imprinted on some kind of sensory-fuckery magic like a hologram. Voct (in a chat PM) wanted to point out how Mr. Headless can't just say his name - he has to say "Mister Headless, spooky Halloween fun for the whole family!" So until someone more astute comes along and makes a better post, my final impression is that this poor guy isn't much of a guy any more, and there's not much of him to sympathise with - as much as one can sympathise with an empty shell. +1 for the implications.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seconding "some kind of amplified photosynthesis" as maybe the best part. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I smiled and upvoted at "some kind of amplified photosynthesis or something", and the subsequent interview did not disappoint.

Particularly, I thought this bit

I probably could've found the house again if there weren't so many screaming people and crashing cars, but, well, I was starting to panic too

painted a vivid picture of this poor guy stumbling around town causing utter chaos.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have a weak spot for the Little Misters, and this one has a fantastic interview to boot. Thanks for doing Mr. Headless well. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you want to commit to the "we don't know if Dr. Wondertainment is an organization, individual, both, or neither," I would replace a few of your "he" and "shes" with "they" maybe even "it".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is a case in which the interview makes, rather than breaks, the SCP. You made me believe in Mr. Headless as a character - and a sympathetic one at that.

You also reinforced my headcanon that Dr. Wondertainment is as twisted as any other GOI.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is immediately one of my favourite LM articles. Like, kaktus said: that interview is really really well written.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've been really dead-set against filling out the Misters, but I have to say, between his personality and the "other people see themselves as headless" effect, this is pretty neat. His backstory makes sense, his confusion about the world is interesting. This adds to the Little Mister canon overall, ergo, I welcome it. :) +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
At first I was wondering why that one side affect of SCP-2287 was needed (the feeling of disconnection and slower movement), since it didn't seem to play into much in the interview log. Then I realized why the car crashes were happening.

(also the rest is p gud)

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: That is a stellar interview.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Credit where it's due: this is based on an original idea and draft by Penton, but I revised and remade it so thoroughly that he said I might as well post it myself.

Notice the confusion about whether Dr Wondertainment is male, or female, or an organization. That's deliberate; in fact, Headless isn't capable of understanding that it's even a question. Consider the phenomenon of tautomerism, and how it might apply to identity.

The thing about years: Headless (as do most, if not all, of the other Little Misters) lives through years several times each, and not necessarily in order. He has no idea that there's anything unusual about this; doesn't everyone do it? How else can you be in more than one place at a time, and do more than one thing at a time? Although lately, it seems to have stopped happening… and if you were to ask the other Little Misters, they'd agree that the last few years have all been consecutive, and only once each, and isn't that strange?

Note that odd naivete about the world. It's not just that he doesn't know the word 'gun', he's missing other details too. But what can you expect of someone who spends >99% of his life in attics and closets and storage compartments?
